content: WEG-25 spike session started
Any struct/enum deriving PartialEq where all fields implement Eq should also derive Eq; clippy::nursery catches this with derive_partial_eq_without_eq.
Error enums in library crates should be #[non_exhaustive] so callers cannot pattern-match all variants exhaustively — adding new error cases later is a non-breaking change.
When a function returns a hardcoded default for a missing file, the unit test should assert the default directly without creating any file — the simplest possible fixture for a missing-resource path.
Mutex::lock() in axum handlers should use match on LockResult and return a 500 response on PoisonError rather than unwrap(); the return statement exits the handler function even from inside a block.
New AgentRoot path helpers follow the pattern: one-line pub fn returning self.parent_dir().join(filename), tested with a hardcoded /tmp/proj fixture asserting the full expected PathBuf.

Pure math functions are exempt from the 3-param grouping heuristic — grouping pain/importance/recurrence into a struct adds boilerplate at every call site without clarifying the formula, especially when the shape is spec-locked.
Use tracing::debug_span!(name, field).entered() at function entry for debug-level spans; binding to _span (not _) keeps the span active until the function returns.

Manual std::error::Error impls need an explicit source() override returning Some(inner_err) for each variant with an underlying error; an empty impl {} silently breaks error chain traversal used by error reporters.

Result/outcome structs with Vec and numeric fields should derive Default when the zero value is semantically meaningful (e.g., DecayResult with no decays) — enables both Default::default() usage and struct update syntax.

Reusing the same binding name (e.g. `e`) across multiple Err arms in a match is not a Rust shadow but is a readability issue; rename to intent-revealing names (not_found, io_err) to prevent confusion, especially when both arms contain error-handling logic.

#[must_use] on a function returning Result forces callers to handle or explicitly ignore the value; the optional string argument (= "...") becomes the warning message, which should explain WHY ignoring is dangerous.
When extracting a TempDir setup helper, return the TempDir as the first tuple element so callers bind it as `_dir` — TempDir drops when it goes out of scope and the helper must not outlive the owner.

Result/outcome structs should expose is_empty() returning true when the zero-work case applies; combine it with #[must_use] so callers who check emptiness get a warning if they silently ignore the result.

3-arm match with NotFound guard cannot be directly replaced with ?; restructure to: call the IO fn, check Err+NotFound with if-let for early Ok return, then use ? on the Result — requires the error type to impl From<io::Error>.

Extract cross-flag validation into a validate() -> Result<(), String> method on Args structs; call it before individual flag checks in the match arm so error messages are consistent and the logic is testable independently of clap.
Display for a newtype wrapping PathBuf should use self.inner_path.display() in write!(); Path::display() returns a Display impl that handles non-UTF-8 paths gracefully, unlike .to_str().unwrap().

Use Path::to_str() + ok_or_else(|| io::Error::new(InvalidData, ..))? for registry/string comparisons; to_string_lossy() silently mangles non-UTF-8 paths with U+FFFD causing silent wrong matches in lookups.

Timestamp fields in state.json are nullable (JSON null before first cycle); read helpers should return Result<Option<String>, WalError> rather than Result<String, WalError> with a fallback string, to distinguish absent-file from present-but-null.

chrono 0.4.44: DateTime::from_timestamp(secs, nsecs) returns Option<DateTime<Utc>> and is NOT deprecated; prefer .unwrap_or_else(|| DateTime::UNIX_EPOCH) over .expect() even for SystemTime-sourced values to avoid any edge-case panics.

cargo test --workspace aggregates across dreamd-protocol (6), dreamd-core (173 lib + 4 integration + 1 weg21), and dreamd (7 cli + 5 init_golden + others); new unit tests added during development increase the baseline — update expected counts in spike reports.
clippy pedantic doc_markdown lint fires when a technical identifier like FastFields appears in a doc comment without backtick wrapping; fix by adding backticks around the identifier
#[non_exhaustive] on pub error enums is essential: forces callers to add a wildcard match arm, so adding variants is non-breaking; DreamCycleError already has it — check before adding
read_last_cycle_status test for missing file already existed from prior session; always grep for existing tests before adding — spike tasks may already be shipped by a prior harness

all post_dream and other http.rs production handlers already use proper error propagation with no .unwrap() calls — a prior sprint already cleaned this up; grep production-only (lines 1 to cfg(test) boundary) to verify
ClusterOutput derives Debug+Clone but not Default; adding Default is safe when all fields have Default impls (Vec<T> does); use cargo test after adding to confirm

consolidation.rs has no variable shadowing within function scopes; the same name (events) appears in separate functions, not nested scopes — not a shadow; verify with clippy shadow lints before assuming shadowing exists

http.rs test module had two tests manually creating TempDir+registry+real-state; extracted real_router_with_dir() helper parallel to existing mock_router_with_dir(); pattern: one helper per state variant (mock vs real coordinator)

three-arm match patterns (Ok, Err(NotFound)=Ok, Err(e)=Err) cannot be replaced with ? because the middle arm changes the Ok type; dreamd-core uses this correctly everywhere — do not flatten these matches

chrono 0.4.44 DateTime::from_timestamp(secs, nsecs) is NOT deprecated — returns Option<Self>; the deprecated versions are timestamp_nanos() (use timestamp_nanos_opt) and date() (use date_naive()); verify in source before migrating

cargo test --workspace passes 217 tests after WEG-25 OpenCode spike session (added cluster_output_default_is_empty in consolidation.rs + real_router_with_dir helper refactor in http.rs tests); expect count to grow each spike session
